description: STATEMENT-format binary logging replicates nondeterministic SQL and drifts the replica.
body: Failure mode: with binlog_format=STATEMENT, statements using NOW(), UUID(), RAND(), or LIMIT without ORDER BY replay differently on the replica, so primary and replica diverge silently. Detection signals: pt-table-checksum reports differing chunks, application reads return stale or different rows from the replica, and the error log shows unsafe-statement warnings. Do not run STATEMENT format with nondeterministic functions. Safer fallback: switch to binlog_format=ROW, re-sync the divergent chunks from the primary with reviewed repair SQL, and re-checksum to confirm zero diffs.
